sentence: Subjective:

HPI: Mr. Nelson, a 73-year-old male, presents with ongoing management of rheumatoid arthritis, specifically complaining of persistent swelling and pain in his right knee, which has been present for approximately two months.
He reports that the swelling started after receiving a steroid injection in the same knee.
He describes his pain level as a 3 out of 10 currently, but typically around 6 on a normal day.
He has been experiencing difficulty getting up from sitting due to stiffness, requiring the use of a cane for support.
Past medical history: Mr. Nelson has a history of rheumatoid arthritis and has been taking Xeljanz 11mg daily and Celebrex 200mg daily for the past two months.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Xeljanz 11mg daily
Celebrex 200mg daily
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's knee is swollen, with pitting around the knee and ankle.
Normal capillary refill in the toes.
Range of motion is slightly diminished.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Mr. Nelson's knee is inflamed, likely due to rheumatoid arthritis, and requires further management.
Problem list: Rheumatoid arthritis; Swelling of the right knee; Knee pain; Limited range of motion in the right knee
Plan:
I prescribed a steroid injection of 80mg of triamcinolone and 1mL of lidocaine to the right knee.
I also ordered an x-ray of the right knee to investigate the cause of the swelling.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help manage his symptoms.
I instructed him to reduce his salt intake and to prop his feet up at night to alleviate edema.
I advised him to follow up with his primary care physician for further management and to schedule a blood work appointment to check his CMP levels.
Follow-up: Mr. Nelson will follow up with his primary care physician for further management and to schedule a blood work appointment to check his CMP levels.
